theoremName: shifted Xi de Branges normalization theorem

normalization.Xi: Xi(z)=xi(1/2+i z), real entire in z

riemannNormalizationInputStatus.label: Riemann/Xi normalization input
riemannNormalizationInputStatus.reason: The Riemann kernel normalization fixes Xi up to harmless positive Gram rescaling.

realEntireSharpStatus.label: real-entire sharp operation
realEntireSharpStatus.reason: Since Xi is real entire in the z-normalization and omega is real, E_omega#(z)=overline(Xi(conj z+i omega))=Xi(z-i omega).

upperHalfPlaneDenominatorStatus.label: upper-half-plane denominator
upperHalfPlaneDenominatorStatus.reason: For z,w in C_+, conj(w)-z cannot vanish.  On the diagonal, 2*pi*i*(conj(z)-z)=4*pi*Im z>0.

canonicalHardyImageInputStatus.label: canonical Hardy/de Branges image input
canonicalHardyImageInputStatus.reason: The hardened Hardy image theorem fixes the same de Branges kernel normalization.

shiftedXiNormalizationStatus.label: shifted-Xi de Branges normalization
shiftedXiNormalizationStatus.reason: The shifted kernel, sharp operation, and diagonal sign convention are all fixed.

proof: Use the fixed Xi normalization from the Riemann kernel theorem.
Use real-entireness of Xi to compute the sharp transform of E_omega.
Check that the de Branges denominator is nonzero on C_+ x C_+ and positive on the diagonal.
Use the canonical Hardy image theorem to match the same kernel normalization.
